Why are you interested in this role?: I'm drawn to this role because it sits right at the intersection of fast-moving product and practical enablement, which has been the core of my work as a Program Enablement Manager. OpenAI ships quickly, and the real challenge isn't the launch—it's helping customers understand what changed, why it matters, and how to get started. That's exactly what I do today: I turn product updates into guides, quickstarts, and webinars, run needs assessments to find where understanding breaks down, and design programs that move people from awareness to adoption. I don't just want to produce content—I want to build the repeatable systems that make OpenAI's newest capabilities genuinely accessible to enterprise customers.
Why OpenAI?: OpenAI's focus on making powerful technology genuinely useful—and widely shared—resonates deeply with how I approach work: relying on data, customer signals, and cross-functional partnership to design education that actually scales. With a master's in Applied Organizational Psychology, I treat learning challenges as systems to be studied, using mixed-methods data to pinpoint capability gaps and build programs that stick. What makes it personal is that I've been building a RAG-based system for myself, so I've felt firsthand how AI can deliver personalized, in-the-moment learning. Ultimately, I want to work at OpenAI because I'm passionate about building human expertise alongside AI technology—helping enterprises learn, adopt, and succeed with frontier tools.
Describe your experience building educat: As a Program Enablement Manager at InGenius Prep, I build customer-facing education end to end. I start with needs assessments across 25+ global stakeholders to find where understanding actually breaks down—I treat assumption as the most expensive thing in L&D—then design programs to close that gap. I've created guides, quickstarts, and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff that cut onboarding time by 75%, and facilitated training webinars for 600+ participants that contributed to a 78% enrollment rate. I coordinate across Sales, Operations, and 10+ vendors, and I measure behavior over completion—are people actually using what we built?—so I can iterate with feedback loops and keep improving the programs over time.
